title: Prunus pseudocerasus watereri
taxonomy: I couldn't find enough information on Prunus pseudocerasus watereri.
constituents: Prunus pseudocerasus watereri is a cultivar of the cherry tree, but unfortunately, information about its specific active constituents is not readily available. However, Prunus species are known to contain various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. These compounds are generally associated with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The specific active constituents of Prunus pseudocerasus watereri may include flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, as well as other phenolic acids. Further research would be necessary to confirm the exact active constituents of this specific cultivar.
intro: Prunus pseudocerasus var. watereri, commonly known as the Chinese cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The health benefits of Chinese cherry are attributed to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, such as amygdalin and prunasin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chinese cherry is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its ability to tonify the spleen and stomach.
